author: @karatepro97
text:
Hi! I just tried intercalating some graphite, but upon heating it with a torch, it didn't expand at all. It just fumed for a second and then nothing more happened. May the reason be that it was still a little damp or I didn`t heat it quickly enough?
By the way, great video!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate - it could be - it could also be your graphite was too small - it needs to be about 50 micrometers or so in size to get really good results otherwise the gasses used to exfoliate the graphite just blow out the sides instead of expanding the layers properly

replies.author: @karatepro97
replies.text:
Thanks for your quick answer! Mine consists of different sizes, but there are still a lot of bigger pieces in there. Tomorrow I'll try again, with hopefully better results. Another thing...Did it ever happen that your graphene solution wasn't black, but light grey? At least the stuff in my sonicator looks like a grey thick cloud just over the graphite. Under the microscope, I saw a few bigger graphite particles, but the rest looked like small, clear "shards". May this be graphene? 
I know this is a lot of text :D

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: well worth trying again mate

author: @greygleam
text: Intercalating with strong acids seems to work fine for making a percursor material to  sonication or shear mixing. Unfortunately strong acids are not avaliable for most  private experimentators esp. the nitric since its deemed an precursor for explosives manufacture.  . LPE by either  sonication or shear mixing seems (at least to me) the most accessible  option for the hobby experimentator since it doesnt involve the use of hard to get and dangerous chemicals.  But the yield rate according to the coleman paper  is quite low, he talks of about 0,1% and with repeated passes of a maximum of 3%  yield rate.  Will using the intercalated ,heat expanded, precursor material significantly increase these yield rates if you use the shear mixing method? Or is it just a method to use a cheaper and more accessible starter material( graphite chunks instead of powder)?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Scullpture I am not sure where you are from mate but you can buy strong acids in the UK and US quite easily - they sell them in the DIY stores as drain and brick cleaner - they are 96% sulphuric and 37% hydrochloric acids respectively. A friend of mine recently bought concentrated nitric acid and it came through the post. It's true you are dealing with chemicals that you have to treat with respect but then the same is true if you use this stuff to clean the drain and that's what they sell it for.

replies.author: @greygleam
replies.text:
+Robert Murray-Smith Thanks for your promt response Robert, I am from the EU. Currently purchase of strong acids is  still possible - even nitric - but its already strongly monitored . At 1.04.16 new regulations will come to effect in the whole of the Eu and purchase of  certain chemicals will be severely restricted. Nitric above 3% strength will not be avaliable to the public anymore. There are even proposals to restrict  the sale of sulphuric acid and acetone among other substances. (FYI : sulfuric acid restriction proposal came from the UK goverment)  . The GO route is  impossible because KMnO4 is already banned here....besides that  I would prefer to  use the method using he least agressive ingredients, so precursor material from intercalated  Graphite would only be interesting if there is a positive difference to  "regular" graphite powder  in terms of yield rate.


you have shown us many viable methods to make our own graphene with simple techniques- kudos to you!! - yours is one of the most informative channels on the subject. I whish you would talk a little more about PROCESSING  the suspended graphene solution.  but I understand these may touch proprietary information. from what i have been gathering from the channel and various papers , the main problem is restacking. if you attempt to just evaporate the graphene suspension you will loose the majority of your hard won graphene to it combining again to larger stacks due to van der waals attraction forces. i have read the paper:. "Prevention of Graphene Restacking for Performance Boost of Supercapacitors—A Review"from here: http://www.mdpi.com/2073-4352/3/1/163 and the simplest route just seems to be keeping it wet. Most of the other methods use either technologies like CVD or chemicals not avaliable to the public. the one other possible method for the hobby experimentor seems to be the introduction of nanoparticle metal oxides like Mno2, Ceo2, Tio2 etc (also could be made by shear mixing) . I see folks on other channels just drying the graphene solution - but not with spectacular results...

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Scullpture I suppose i don't hit this problem much as I am a registered lab - but as you say there are a lot of methods using harmless chemicals and i will do a highlight vido on this soon along with some processing ideas - but you are right the simplest method is to keep it in solution  until you need it

author: @JonathanFosdickNano
text:
That's a brilliant way to expand graphite! (Pun intended :p)

On your video you mentioned "bisulphate" as an intercalating species, are you referring to SBS (Sodium bisulphate)? I ask as I am working to refine my process in which I use concentrated sulphuric and phosphoric acids to acid treat the flake graphite. Microwave radiation will cut the graphene apart in a 9:1 H2SO4/HNO3 acid solution, and the arcing between graphene planes produces the light. But what I wanted to know is where would you suggest I can purchase the large flake graphite from?  Could I purchase the graphite crystals directly from you or the FWG? 

Lol, you should acquire or build for yourself a fume hood / cabinet as the acid fumes will permeate your lab and wreak havoc and on your electrics and your computer, etc.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Jonathan Fosdick I don't do enough of this kind of thing to really worry too much about it mate - but  fume hood is a must - i don't sell the large flake i am afraid - but there are suppliers on the net - fortunately i get mine as a freebie
